pages.text: The Isotope Centrifuge can enrich Uranium by separating U-235 from U-238. However, a single stage only provides a small boost. You may need multiple stages or a cascade to reach high enrichment levels. Enriched UF6 and tails (depleted UF6) are outputted and should be recycled to maximize efficiency.

The enrichment process uses Uranium Hexafluoride (UF6) gas. The centrifuge splits 2 units of input into 1 unit of slightly enriched product and 1 unit of slightly depleted tails. This mass-conservative process requires a cascade of machines to reach higher enrichment levels.
